lenses.description: The widest lens in Zeiss's Compact Prime CP.2 line, a full-frame cine prime at T2.9. Like the rest of the CP.2 set it uses an interchangeable mount that swaps between PL, EF, F, E and MFT, so a single lens serves both cine and stills bodies without adapters. A 14-blade iris keeps out-of-focus highlights round across the aperture range, and the T* coating with internal light traps holds flare down. Focus and iris are manual, on a long focus rotation.

It uses the compact CP.3 housing with a 95mm front, a 14-blade iris for round bokeh, and the interchangeable mount (PL, EF, F, E, MFT) with an eXtended Data option. Focus is manual on a smooth 300-degree rotation, with minimal breathing and the clean Zeiss look.

lenses.description: The widest lens in Zeiss's CP.3 line, a compact full-frame prime at T2.9. The CP.3 set is the third-generation Compact Prime, smaller and lighter than the CP.2 while still covering full frame, with a 14-blade iris that keeps out-of-focus highlights round through the T-stop range. All ten share a 95mm front and matched gear positions, and the interchangeable mount swaps between PL, EF, F, E and MFT, with an eXtended Data option that transmits lens metadata over Cooke /i and Zeiss datasets. Focus is manual on a smooth 300-degree rotation.

lenses.description: The wide zoom in Zeiss's Compact Zoom CZ.2 line, a full-frame cine zoom holding a constant T2.9 across a 15-30mm range. Like the rest of the CZ.2 set it is parfocal, keeping focus through the zoom, and covers full frame for large-sensor cameras. It uses Zeiss's interchangeable mount so a single lens serves cine and DSLR bodies, with the clean Zeiss rendering and eXtended Data metadata. A useful wide-to-normal range for run-and-gun and documentary work.

It holds focus through the zoom and covers full frame, on Zeiss's interchangeable mount for use across cine and DSLR bodies. The clean Zeiss rendering and eXtended Data metadata match the rest of the CZ.2 set, making the 28-80 a versatile everyday zoom.

lenses.description: The telephoto zoom in Zeiss's Compact Zoom CZ.2 line, a full-frame parfocal cine zoom at a constant T2.9 across 70-200mm. It keeps focus through the zoom and covers full frame, on the interchangeable mount shared across the CZ.2 set. With Zeiss rendering and eXtended Data, it handles the long end for interviews, wildlife and coverage where reach and shallow depth of field matter.

lenses.description: Zeiss's Lightweight Zoom LWZ.2, a compact Super 35 cine zoom covering 15.5-45mm at a constant T2.6. Built light for documentary, ENG and gimbal work, it is parfocal, holding focus through the zoom, on a PL mount. It offers a useful wide-to-normal range with the Zeiss rendering in a package sized for run-and-gun shooting.

lenses.description: Zeiss's Lightweight Zoom LWZ.3, a compact Super 35 cine zoom with a wide 21-100mm range, nearly 5x. Its aperture ramps across the zoom, from T2.9 at the wide end to T3.9 at full telephoto, and it is parfocal, holding focus through the range on a PL mount. Light enough for documentary, ENG and gimbal setups, it trades a little speed at the long end for reach and portability.

lenses.description: A short-telephoto full-frame prime in Zeiss's Supreme Prime line, fast at T1.5. It keeps the line's internal focus design for minimal breathing and a non-rotating front, with the same 16-blade iris for round out-of-focus highlights. Color and rendering stay matched to the other Supremes, and the gears sit in the standard positions for fast lens changes. The field-interchangeable mount ships in PL and passes metadata via Cooke /i and Zeiss eXtended Data.

lenses.description: The widest focal length in Zeiss's Supreme Prime line, a full-frame cine prime that opens to a fast T1.8. An internal focus design keeps breathing minimal and the front non-rotating, and a 16-blade iris renders round out-of-focus highlights. It is color-matched to the rest of the Supreme set and shares the same gear positions, so it drops in without re-rigging, and it carries lens metadata over Cooke /i and Zeiss eXtended Data. The wide optics use a larger front than the middle of the set, and the mount is a field-interchangeable PL.

lenses.description: The long end of Zeiss's Supreme Prime Radiance line, a full-frame telephoto prime still fast at T1.5. Like the rest of the Radiance set it is a Supreme Prime with the T* blue coating for controlled, planned flares and a slightly warmer look, while retaining the Supreme optics: 46.3mm full-frame coverage, a 16-blade iris for smooth bokeh, a 300-degree focus rotation and eXtended Data. With the 18mm it carries the line's larger 114mm front rather than the 95mm used across the middle of the range, and it suits large-format portraiture and isolation.

lenses.description: The wide end of Zeiss's Supreme Prime Radiance line, a full-frame cine prime at a fast T1.5. The Radiance lenses are the Supreme Primes rebuilt with the T* blue coating, which lets a cinematographer create controlled, repeatable flares under directed light while keeping contrast and transmission intact, with a slightly warmer rendering than the standard Supremes. It keeps the Supreme optics otherwise: 46.3mm full-frame coverage, a 16-blade iris for smooth bokeh, a 300-degree focus rotation and eXtended Data metadata. Along with the 135mm it uses a larger 114mm front, the two exceptions to the line's 95mm standard.

lenses.description: The wide zoom in Zeiss's Supreme Zoom Radiance line, a full-frame parfocal cine zoom at a constant T2.9 across 15-30mm. These are the only zooms built to match the Supreme Prime Radiance look: the T* blue coating produces controlled, reproducible blue flares under directed light and a slightly warmer rendition, while normal lighting renders cleanly. The flare shape even shifts as you zoom, and the lens holds focus through the range. It carries the Radiance line's 114mm front, matched gear positions and eXtended Data metadata.

lenses.description: The standard zoom in Zeiss's Supreme Zoom Radiance line, a full-frame parfocal cine zoom at a constant T2.9 across 28-80mm. Like the set it uses the T* blue coating for controlled, planned flares and a warmer look, rendering normally under regular light, and it holds focus through the zoom. It matches the Supreme Prime Radiance and Supreme Primes for gear position and look, with a 114mm front and eXtended Data. It is the lightest of the three Radiance zooms.

lenses.description: The telephoto zoom in Zeiss's Supreme Zoom Radiance line, a full-frame parfocal cine zoom at a constant T2.9 across 70-200mm. It shares the T* blue coating for controlled blue flares and a warmer rendition, rendering cleanly under normal light, and holds focus through the zoom. Matched to the Supreme Prime Radiance and Supreme Primes in gear position and look, it carries the 114mm front and eXtended Data, and is the heaviest of the three Radiance zooms.
